  • Changelog

    Module: cor_nonlinear

    module-cor-nonlinear.Rmd

    Purpose

    cor_nonlinear fits nonlinear (spline-based) dose-response models relating immunological marker levels to COVID-19 risk. It models the relationship between continuous marker values and vaccine efficacy using Super Learner ensemble methods, and renders marginalized risk curves.

    Source module: correlates_reporting2/cor_nonlinear/
    Key report: cor_nonlinear/cor_nonlinear_report.Rmd

    Example data 

    report_root  <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
moderna_path <- file.path(
  report_root, "data_clean",
  "moderna_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv"
)

    Reproduce the report

    1. Configure the session 

    library(utilrep2)

report_root <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
setup_session(
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = file.path(report_root, "data_clean"),
  report_root = report_root
)

    2. Run the module 

    run_cor_nonlinear(trial = "moderna_mock")

    For moderna_mock this runs cor_nonlinear.R for D57 then D29.

    After the analysis, report = TRUE (the default) renders cor_nonlinear_report.Rmd to a trial-labelled PDF:

    # cor_nonlinear_report_moderna_mock.pdf
    run_cor_nonlinear(trial = "moderna_mock", report = FALSE)

    Output

    Results and plots are saved under cor_nonlinear/output/<trial>/.

    Report sections (from cor_nonlinear_report.Rmd)

    • Nonlinear risk curves per marker and timepoint
    • Comparison of Super Learner learner contributions
    • Risk plots overlaid with VE estimates

    Supported trials

    Trial family CoR timepoints
    moderna_mock, moderna_real D57, D29
    janssen_pooled_mock / EUA variants D29, D29start1
    hvtn705 variants D210
    prevent19 D35

    Developed by Y H.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
